questions.question: Explain why India is considered a country with diverse physical features.
questions.answer: India has diverse physical features including mountains, plains, plateaus, deserts, coastal plains and islands which differ in relief and formation.

questions.question: Describe the main physiographic divisions of India.
questions.answer: India has six major physiographic divisions: the Himalayan Mountains, the Northern Plains, the Peninsular Plateau, the Indian Desert, the Coastal Plains and the Islands.

questions.question: Explain the geographical extent of the Himalayan Mountains.
questions.answer: The Himalayas extend from the Indus river in the west to the Brahmaputra river in the east forming a 2400 km long arc across northern India.

questions.question: Describe the three parallel ranges of the Himalayas.
questions.answer: The Himalayan system consists of Himadri, Himachal and Shiwalik ranges each with different height and characteristics.

questions.question: Describe the features of the Himachal Himalayas.
questions.answer: The Himachal Himalayas consist of rugged mountains, valleys and famous hill stations like Shimla and Mussoorie.

questions.question: Explain the formation of the Northern Plains.
questions.answer: The Northern Plains were formed by alluvial deposits brought by the Indus, Ganga and Brahmaputra river systems over millions of years.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the Northern Plains in India.
questions.answer: They provide fertile soil, support agriculture and are densely populated regions.

questions.question: Explain the four relief divisions of the Northern Plains.
questions.answer: The Northern Plains are divided into Bhabar, Terai, Bhangar and Khadar based on relief features and soil type.

questions.question: Describe the Central Highlands of the Peninsular Plateau.
questions.answer: Central Highlands lie north of the Narmada river and include the Malwa plateau and Bundelkhand region.

questions.question: Describe the Deccan Plateau.
questions.answer: The Deccan Plateau lies south of the Narmada river and forms a triangular landmass between the Western and Eastern Ghats.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the Peninsular Plateau.
questions.answer: It is rich in mineral resources and supports mining and industrial development.

questions.question: Explain the Western Ghats and their importance.
questions.answer: Western Ghats run parallel to the western coast and cause heavy rainfall due to orographic effect.

questions.question: Explain the Eastern Ghats.
questions.answer: Eastern Ghats are discontinuous mountains along the eastern side of the plateau cut by rivers.

questions.question: Describe the Indian Desert and its features.
questions.answer: The Indian Desert lies west of the Aravali Hills and has sand dunes, arid climate and low rainfall.

questions.question: Explain the climate of the Indian Desert.
questions.answer: The region experiences very low rainfall, high temperatures and sparse vegetation.

questions.question: Describe the western coastal plains.
questions.answer: They lie between the Western Ghats and Arabian Sea and include Konkan, Kannad and Malabar coasts.

questions.question: Describe the eastern coastal plains.
questions.answer: Eastern coastal plains lie between the Eastern Ghats and Bay of Bengal and contain large river deltas.

questions.question: Explain why eastern coastal plains are wider than western coastal plains.
questions.answer: Because large rivers like Mahanadi, Godavari, Krishna and Kaveri form extensive deltas there.

questions.question: Explain the importance of coastal plains.
questions.answer: Coastal plains support fishing, agriculture, tourism and major ports.

questions.question: Describe the Lakshadweep Islands.
questions.answer: Lakshadweep islands are small coral islands located in the Arabian Sea near Kerala.

questions.question: Describe the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
questions.answer: These islands lie in the Bay of Bengal and form an elongated chain of islands with dense forests.

questions.question: Explain the strategic importance of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
questions.answer: Their location near important sea routes makes them strategically important for security.

questions.question: Explain the importance of mountains in India's geography.
questions.answer: Mountains provide water sources, forests and influence climate.

questions.question: Explain the importance of plateaus in India.
questions.answer: Plateaus provide minerals, support industries and influence river systems.

questions.question: Explain how India's physical features influence its economy.
questions.answer: Mountains provide water, plains support farming, plateaus provide minerals and coasts support trade.
